content.claim: Between-school variance in Hungary ranks third-highest among OECD countries, while socio-economic background accounts for 23.1% of student performance variance compared to the OECD average of 14.8%, indicating the Hungarian system produces significant equity disparities.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Specific OECD publication date and methodology not provided in research notes
Within-school variance is below OECD average, suggesting complexity in interpreting between-school effects
Causality between tracking and SES variance not directly tested in cited analysis
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
